name: NOAA Southwest Fisheries Science Center (example)
summary: Federal science center that conducts research on living marine resources and their habitats along the U.S. West Coast and in the Pacific; central to U.S. fisheries and aquatic ecosystem management.

name: U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service (example row)
summary: Federal agency whose primary work is wildlife conservation, recovery of at-risk species, and stewardship of the National Wildlife Refuge System and other public lands and waters in partnership with states and Tribes.

name: The Nature Conservancy (example row)
summary: Large non-governmental organization that protects lands and waters through science, voluntary stewardship, and policy partnerships; a typical conservation NGO entry for the directory.

name: Washington Department of Fish and Wildlife (example row)
summary: State natural resource agency responsible for managing fish, wildlife, and their habitats in Washington; an exemplar of a state fish and game / wildlife department entry.
